title.rendered: Brits Snap Up Bargain Turkish Holidays as the Lira Crashes

content.rendered:
Turkey has emerged as one of the best-priced getaway options for British holidaymakers this year with the value of the lira plummeting.

Brits can get a whopping 20 lira for one pound right now. There are many reports of families of four spending less than £850 on food and spending money for a week on holiday in Turkey.

If you have booked a Turkish getaway in the coming weeks and months, it is certainly worth considering ordering your lira now. You could save yourself hundreds of pounds by taking advantage of the weak lira.

Further savings with off-season travelling

The Turkish currency has dropped in value significantly over the last few weeks, which means you could get your holiday for half or a quarter of the price with the current rates. And, if you are not bound by school holidays, then Turkey could be an excellent, value-for-money winter break in the sun for you.

Order now to use later

If you introduce just a little bit of organisation and order your currency now through a trusted source like TravelFX, then you will have it ready for your winter, spring or even summer holidays.

Turkey has become a firm favourite with British holidaymakers with its world-class beaches, stunning, ancient historical ruins and wonderful climate.

The cost of a sunshine Turkish holiday is in stark contrast to reports of holidaymakers who decided to stay in the UK. Many have reported that even budget camping trips in favourite British holiday spots were costing hundreds of pounds more than a family trip to Turkey.

In recent surveys of spending habits of holidaymakers visiting Turkey, it was reported that most people were not spending more than £20 each per day on food, travel and sightseeing. Meals for the whole day wouldn’t total more than £6 per person and local travel would be around £3 per person.

Good News for Turkey bound travellers

This good news for Turkey bound travellers comes after the British government also lifted travel warnings against the country earlier in the summer.

Most holidaymakers jet out to resorts in the west of Turkey, like Bodrum, Antalya and Istanbul, while the bans were for provinces bordering Syria in the east. But it is still reassuring for travellers to Turkey that it is deemed a safe country to visit.

Top Tip: If you do decide to travel to Turkey on holiday, it is important that you carry your passport with you everywhere you go. It is illegal not to carry some kind of photo ID with you. The local authorities often undertake spot checks on foreign visitors and you risk being fined if you are not carrying your ID.

Get Lira delivered to your door

TravelFX offers a slick online ordering service and will deliver your currency directly to your door using Royal Mail Special Delivery. As well as Turkish Lira, you can find the very best rates for Euros, US Dollars, Australian Dollars, UAE Dirham, Thai Bhat and many other currencies from around the world. You can order your holiday cash securely here:

itemListElement.name: Brits Snap Up Bargain Turkish Holidays as the Lira Crashes

@graph.name: Travel FX Blog
@graph.description: Travel money news and tips from Travel FX

@graph.name: Lucy Reed
@graph.description: Hi! I’m Lucy, as well as writing blog posts I lead Customer Experience and UX at Travel FX. With over 15 years’ experience in the foreign exchange industry, I specialise in helping UK travellers understand exchange rates, avoid unnecessary fees, and feel confident about their travel money before they fly. Having lived both in the UK and overseas, and travelled extensively over the years, I understand first-hand how important it is to get the details right when you’re planning a trip. From managing your travel budget to choosing the right foreign currency or travel money card, small decisions can make a big difference.
